column1: Data Supplied By: National Livestock Reporting Service
Data source: National Livestock Reporting Service
Please see terms of use here: https://www.mla.com.au/general/Terms-and-conditions/data-and-information/

Numbers were up by nearly 400 head which saw Singleton agents pen 1486 cattle, quality was very good, as were the prices. 

Big runs of steer and heifer calves saw buyers able to secure numbers and resulted in light steer calves improving by 21c/kg making from 405c to 620c/kg with medium and heavy weights over 330kg to both restock and feed sell between 480c and 586c/kg. Numbers of heifer calves nearly doubled their steer counterparts to be 30c/kg dearer selling from 350c to 514c/kg averaging around 490c/kg.

There were some exceptional supplementary fed B muscle steer and heifer calves and attracted strong competition from the trade buyers to be 26c/kg dearer making from 554c to 578c/kg. Quality related rises in medium and heavy yearling steers to feed on and sold between 410c and 538c/kg. Too few yearling heifers to gain a reliable trend but the heavy weights over 400kg to the lot feeders topped at 536c/kg.

Just on 200 cows were offered, lighter 2 scores sold from 240c to 345c/kg, the 3 and 4 score heavy weight cows held firm to 2c/kg dearer and traded from 350c to 420c/kg.

Market Operator Angus Barlow.
